compiled_state.title: Opus SDET review loop: review tests, remediate important/critical findings, repeat until clean
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
A senior-SDET-quality review of the test suite, run as an iterative loop, is the closing quality gate for the post-migration hardening.

## What
- Run an opus subagent primed as an SDET to review the project's testing (coverage adequacy, assertion quality, isolation, flakiness, fixture design, seam organization, contract coverage across the 3 interfaces, CI wiring).
- Remediate every important/critical finding.
- Repeat the review→remediate loop until a review returns NO important/critical findings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] At least one opus SDET-primed review performed and its findings recorded
- [ ] All important/critical findings remediated (with tests/changes referenced)
- [ ] Review loop repeated until a clean pass (no important/critical findings)
- [ ] Suite green locally + CI after remediation

comments.body:
SDET REVIEW ROUND 1 (opus) — suite broadly healthy + unusually disciplined on isolation (root conftest: repo-root/HEAD guards, porcelain backstop, socket guard, external-marker confinement; signing is the strongest area). Concentrated findings to remediate:
CRITICAL:
- C1: link echo-suppression/dedup has ZERO deterministic CI coverage — only exercised in gated external tests. Production dedup (outbound_differ._diff_links; inbound_differ._diff_links_inbound) untested in CI. Add unit tests in tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/: (a) local blocks dep already in Jira issuelinks -> _diff_links returns []; (b) inbound issuelink already in local deps -> [] ; (c) 6th roundtrip case in test_reconcile_roundtrip.py. ALSO: test_link_sync.py docstring L20-24 is STALE/FALSE (claims links hardcoded [] / no InboundMutation.links) — rewrite.
- C2: duplicate-link-on-retry untested + code admits it's deferred. Add set_relationship side_effect=[URLError,None] test asserting at-most-once (call_count==1 / pre-create check); xfail(strict) until d843 fix. Add call_count==1 to happy path.
- C3: test_conflict_resolver_echo_suppression.py tautological — never inspects compute_mutations return; assert pass2==[] / echo absent from returned list.
- C4: fork/union tie-break (test_concurrency_regression.py:207) asserts set-membership not the specific UUID winner — derive expected winner by the documented UUID rule and assert it; assert BOTH CREATE files survive union.
IMPORTANT: I1 inbound apply over-mocks rebar.link (add real tmp_path store test + invalid-relation rejection); I2 parity adapters collapse rejection to bare bool (assert CLI exit-10 + MCP tool-error same identity); I3 MCP concurrency identity is prose-substring not typed code; I4 forward-compat asserts file exists only (assert payload preserved); I5 jitter test over-pins magic constants; I6 /tmp global sync markers not namespaced; I7 raw monkeypatch without restore.
TOP 5: C1, C3, C2, C4, I2/I3. This drives remediation round 1.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: SDET REVIEW ROUND 2 (closing gate) — opus, verified against actual code not just claims. All round-1 CRITICAL (C1 link dedup CI coverage, C2 duplicate-on-retry, C3 tautological echo test, C4 fork tie-break) and IMPORTANT (I1 inbound real-store, I2/I3 shared concurrency identity, I4 forward-compat payload, I5/I6/I7 isolation/brittleness) findings RESOLVED. d843 subprocess-reap tests judged robust (real grandchild-holding-pipe reap, AcliTimeoutError-not-TimeoutError guard, read/write classification). Independent scan found NO new important/critical issues (signing/apply/concurrency/parity all assert observable behavior). Sanity run green (broad 1812). Non-blocking: bug 8874 (reducer UUID-vs-filename tie-break doc mismatch; clones still converge) tracked separately. VERDICT: ZERO important/critical findings remain — gate PASSES. The SDET loop is complete.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
